calendar.matters:
PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Dec-14-2016 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Daniel Mitchell (via Court Call) in Propria Persona. Carol Mitchell (mother of decedent) in Propria Persona.

The Court asked Mr. Mitchell if he had received the examiner's notes, that the examiner reviewed the petition and pointed out the issues. Mr. Mitchell replied that he had not and that he thought the issues from May & June had been resolved. The Court pointed out the specific issues and additional documents that were needed. Mr. Mitchell stated that he had filed notice on what was put in the newspaper. The Court stated that the issues have not been addressed, that this is the third continuance due to the issues, that unless the problems are cured the Court will take the petition off calendar and that the examiner pointed out another avenue that could be taken. Mr. Mitchell stated he is concerned that the 13100 might make his mother a target for creditors who will sue her, that they are aware of 3 creditors and that they intend to pay them with the assets. The Court stated that there could be creditor issues regardless of how you proceed and that it is not easy to comply with the requirements when in Pro Per. Mr. Mitchell stated that it may be easier to go with the 13100, to pay the creditors and deal with other creditors if it comes up and that he agrees to take the Petition for Letters of Administration off calendar.

Judge: Paul Alvarado. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Chan. (Not Reported)
